name: PR Review
description: Transcribed as data (#1284 Packet 1) from build_review_graph (crates/darkmux-lab/src/lab/review.rs), the Task/Step graph `darkmux pr-review run` builds: investigate (resolve bundles from the diff, probe every staffed seat, dedup the flags) -> adjudicate (double-confirm judge over the deduped flags) -> report (verify confirmed findings, synthesize tier counts). As of Packet 3, this document is the EXECUTABLE source of truth -- build_review_graph is a thin launcher that loads this config, resolves crew_staffing into the probe-template task's `expand` collection, and calls mission_config::interpret.

inputs.name: diff
inputs.description: The unified diff text under review (ReviewStepContext.diff). Fed to every probe/judge/verify dispatch.

inputs.name: case_id
inputs.description: Unique id for this review run (ReviewStepContext.case_id) -- correlates flow records and the review envelope.

inputs.name: intent_title
inputs.description: PR title, gives probes/judges the change's stated intent (ReviewStepContext.intent_title).

inputs.name: crew_staffing
inputs.description: Resolved probe/judge/verify seat staffing (CrewStaffingSnapshot / ResolvedSeatStaffing) from the operator's crew config. Determines HOW MANY review.probe:<seat-name> tasks the investigate phase's probe-template task expands into at launch time -- see the 'review-probe-template' task's notes below. This is the single biggest thing this schema cannot express yet: a config-time repeat-per-staffed-seat primitive does not exist (#1284 Packet 1 finding; Packet 3 must decide how a launcher expands this task).

inputs.name: judge_concurrency
inputs.description: The judge step's real config.concurrency, resolved by the launcher from darkmux_types::config_access::review_judge_concurrency() (env > config.review.judge_concurrency > 1). ALWAYS supplied by the launcher as a step_config_override on review-judge-step -- the static `config.concurrency: 1` below is a documented DEFAULT for a human reading this file, never load-bearing at launch (the launcher always resolves+overrides it, it never reads the static value back out of this document).

phases.description: resolve review bundles from the diff, probe every staffed seat, dedup

tasks.description: resolve review bundles from the diff

TEMPLATE for the probe stage. build_review_graph builds ONE task/step PER STAFFED PROBE SEAT (kind `review.probe:<seat-name>`, ids `review-probe-{index}-task` / `review-probe-{index}-step`) -- a count and a set of names only known once `crew_staffing` (see inputs) resolves. The `expand` block below (#1284 Packet 3, schema 1.1) is the typed, interpretable primitive mission_config::interpret uses to turn this ONE template into N real tasks at launch -- the launcher resolves `probe_seats` from crew_staffing's staffed probe seats (in staffing order) and passes it as a LaunchParams.expansions entry.
dedup probe flags -- mechanism-family keying + anchor matching
double-confirm judge -- internal pass1/pass2 loop over deduped flags

phases.description: verify confirmed findings, synthesize tier counts

notes: always present in the graph even when no verify seat is staffed -- ReviewVerifyStepKind itself no-ops on an absent seat; this config does not (yet) express that conditionality.

tasks.description: synthesis -- finalize tier counts + needs-check clustering
